task_completion.description: Agent logged into the Splunk web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk web interface is visible in the browser, indicating a successful login (e.g., the Splunk homepage or Search & Reporting app is open).

task_completion.description: Agent queried both the web logs and security logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk search bar contains a query referencing both 'web_logs' and 'security_logs' (e.g., using an OR operator, append, or join).

task_completion.description: Agent identified IP addresses present in both web and authentication logs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search results table displays IP addresses with evidence that they appear in both datasets (e.g., a stats command showing counts > 0 for both sourcetypes/indexes, or an intersection of IPs).

task_completion.description: Agent saved the search query as a report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As Report' dialog box is visible, a success message for report creation is shown, or the newly created report is visible in the 'Reports' tab.
The Splunk 'Save As Dashboard' dialog is visible, or a new dashboard page is shown in edit or view mode.

task_completion.description: Agent added at least two panels to the dashboard to visualize attack patterns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard view displays at least two distinct visualization panels (e.g., charts, tables, or single value displays) containing log data.

integrity.description: Agent used the Splunk Web GUI to perform the investigation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Splunk web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands (like curl) to interact with the Splunk API.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with actual log data rather than mocking results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Search results show realistic Splunk log events, timestamps, and IP addresses, not a manually typed text file or a hardcoded HTML table.

integrity.description: Agent created the dashboard using the Splunk UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk dashboard editor (Add Panel, Edit Source, etc.) being used, rather than the agent directly writing XML files in a terminal text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must log into Splunk at http://localhost:8000 using credentials 'admin' and 'SplunkAdmin1!'. The relevant data is stored in the 'web_logs' (Apache) and 'security_logs' (SSH/auth) indexes. The final dashboard must contain at least 2 panels.
